reasoning: The document contains multiple major structural defects. Both LLM and programmatic signals corroborate that large tables spanning multiple pages with repeated headers were incorrectly split into separate table elements. Additionally, programmatic detectors found that footnotes were incorrectly converted to bulleted lists. Since there are 1-5 major defects, the document must be routed to HUMAN_REVIEW.

concerns.analysis: The source document contains several large tables that span multiple pages with repeated headers (e.g., 'Number of Coastal Recreational Fishing Licenses sold by North Carolina county of sale' on pages 4-6, state of residence on pages 10-11, and country of residence on pages 12-15). According to the extraction rules, when a large table spans multiple PDF pages with repeated headers, it must be rendered as one continuous table. However, the output JSON separates these into individual table objects
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The table 'Number of Coastal Recreational Fishing Licenses sold by North Carolina county of sale' spans across pages 4, 5, and 6 as a single continuous data set.
concerns.output_quote: The JSON output extracts this as three completely separate table objects within the page 4, page 5, and page 6 arrays.

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 6 table(s) with repeated headers across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=6. Examples: pages 5-6: 6 cols (, 2010, 2011...); pages 7-8: 6 cols (county, 2010, 2011...); pages 10-11: 6 cols (state, 2010, 2011...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements with duplicated header rows. The HTML output will show multiple tables instead of one continuous table — a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 1 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=1. Examples: 1 item(s): "DSS - Department of Social Services issues free su". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 16 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=16. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

action_items: Merge split tables across consecutive pages into single continuous table objects.
Convert list items acting as footnotes back to proper paragraph elements.
Verify and add missing <th> semantic tags to table headers.
